rationale: Wrap the implication-shaped postcondition in `assert(LHS ==> det_equal) by { if LHS { ... } }`, case-split on the concrete `pkt.msg` (bridging to the abstract `SingleMessage` variant), and in each branch pin the abstract post-state to a function of `(pre_self_@, pkt@)` via `Map::ext_equal` (`=~=`); rule out impossible result variants of r1/r2 with the singleton-vs-empty `get_abstracted_ack_set()` contradiction. The two final asserts on `epmap == epmap` surface the genuine residual gap: `HashMap<V>` is `external_body` with an `uninterp` `view`, so view-equality cannot soundly be lifted to opaque-HashMap equality (compounded by the `..post` instead of `..pre` quirk in `receive_ack`'s spec, which leaves `receive_state` unconstrained in the Ack update branch).
